content:
{
  "title": "Chain-of-Error-Prompts: Leveraging Iterative Bug Injection for Enhanced Code Generation in LLMs",
  "problem": "Large language models for code generation frequently produce syntactically correct but semantically buggy programs, especially on complex tasks. Existing prompting methods insufficiently guide models to anticipate and repair common coding errors.",
  "motivation": "As code generation LLMs become increasingly deployed in production and education, reliability and correctness are paramount. Recent advances in prompting (e.g., chain-of-thought, self-consistency) improve reasoning but are not tailored to the unique challenges of code correctness. A prompting method that systematically steers models to reason about and fix likely errors could vastly improve output quality.",
  "method": "We propose Chain-of-Error Prompting (CoE): for a given coding task, the prompt first instructs the model to generate a naive solution, then explicitly asks it to predict, enumerate, and explain potential bugs in that solution, and finally to provide a corrected version. This structured prompt sequence is designed to induce the model to perform error analysis and self-repair, leveraging its internal knowledge of common coding mistakes. The method is template-agnostic and can be layered on top of few-shot or zero-shot settings.",
  "experiment_plan": "We evaluate CoE on the HumanEval and MBPP code generation benchmarks, comparing against chain-of-thought and standard instruction prompting as baselines. Pass@k and functional correctness will be measured using the official eval harnesses. The experiment will focus on models at or above the GPT-3.5 scale to assess scalability and generality."
}
